User comment: Gorgeous, Modest Evening Dress! I Sized Down For A Fitted Look. Absolutely stunning modest evening dress!! This dress is PERFECT! I love everything about it! Great length (just a tad bit longer than I'd like, but I'm 5ft.3in. Great length with high heels on though), beautiful details, flowing sleeves and skirt and it's modest! So hard to find flattering modest dresses and this one checks all the boxes for me! (except for nursing-friendly! haha) I usually wear a size 6, but I sized down to a size 4, for a more fitted look and it feels like it was made customized for me! It fits me so perfectly and it isn't tight! Perfect dress! Buy it, you won't regret it! Highly recommend!

User comment: Great summer dress This is a really fun, unique.. yet classic looking dress. You could easily dress it up or down, and it's just in time for summer. The material is a nice satin, the color a beautiful ivory/beige, and the way it ties gives you some room for how it fits. There is a zipper and elastic waist in the back. Tying it takes a bit of a learning curve. .. but once you get the hang of it, it looks good. For size reference I'm 5'6" 135lbs 34D, 29" high waist 39" low hips.

User comment: I was looking for a modest swimsuit to wear for church camps as a youth leader and I came across this beautiful swim dress! I really liked the pattern, shape of the neck line and the cute tie up detailing! The one thing I wish it had was a longer torso with the cup pads a bit higher. It felt like I was fighting to get a bit of coverage up top and lift. The skirt portion also has an interesting sewn seam. Nothing super out there, but I did find it a bit strange and with the color of the suit, the white thread did stand out a bit. Overall, the suit was well made and comfortable. I got lots of compliments from the other young gals at my church! I didn't see women in there 20's without kids posting reviews, so here I am, helping a sister out! I got a size Small, I'm about 127-132lbs, athletic build, and a 32D. Hope this helps someone else!

User comment: Update: Still LOVE this suit! It's 7+ months later, and a full summer of boating & swimming has gone by. And the suit held up well, and still fits well, even as I'velost 110+ lbs. Edges are still fine. Colors are still bright. Suit is extremely comfortable to wear all day on the water. I'll be ordering a new one soon in a different style for next summer season. Just came in today. Super cute & fits great! It's the 5th suit I've bought this season, and first one I'll be able to keep. Yeah! I'm 5ft 2 in, & weigh 203#. I wear size 16 regulars & XL in clothes, but always buy a size 18 bathing suit. After returning 3 others (and not able to fit comfortably in the 4th suit after washing), I decided to try this 18W. My measurements are 46 bust, 43 waist, 46 hips, so I was worried the waist might be a bit snug since the sizing chart says it's a 39.5 inch waist, and I would need a 20W for me. But my bust & hips were middle of the range for the 18W. The flowing A-style design flows down from just under the bust & flows over the waist & belly. Shorts are well-fitting too, with plenty of coverage all around & not tight at all. The back is traditional straight line, not too high for me. Straps are medium width, and the girls are well-supported. It's a printed design (on one side only), printed onto white material. Colors are vibrant. The edge is NOT hemmed / sewed. I don't like that, but have struggled to find anything with a sewn hem. So decided to give this try after reading thousands of reviews. I may take it to my local tailor to see if she'll sew it for me. Until then, I'll see how well it holds up in the wash.

User comment: Not for taller people My first impression when putting these pants on were regarding the fabric. Buttery soft and very comfortable. I actually kept them on all day because they feel like yoga pants. When it comes to wearing them as slacks, a few things to know…they have a working button, zipper and pockets. So they feel like legit work pants. But much comfier since I could just slip them on without unzipping. They have a lot of stretch to them which is great. I think they are more meant to be worn with a shirt tucked in because the pockets are high up on the high waist rather than on the sides. So they'd be trickier to access with a shirt untucked. The inseam was perfect on me when I pulled them up to the waist. But then the hem was too high for me at 5'7". And pulling them down to make them longer made the crotch too low. So maybe they're better for someone a few inches shorter. But with the right shoe, it probably wouldn't matter. Overall they're a great pant. Just wish they were longer. I'll be wearing them with an untucked shirt just because the fabric does hug the body enough to show your shape and I'm not crazy about mine. For reference, I am 165 pounds. Waist is 35" and hip is 42" and I got an XL.

User comment: Okay, let me start out with saying my husband bought one of these dresses for me in a different color, because he knew I was struggling with finding flattering things for my current body shape. When I tried it on, both of our jaws dropped, it was absolutely perfect!! Flattering, but covers the areas you want covered! A lot of dresses that ‘hide' the tummy area are boxy or baggy and unflattering! Not the case with this! I am 5'4 and about 175-180 for reference. Smaller boobs, frumpy tummy and I have a big butt and hips. But it stays up even with a smaller chest! Now, onto the picture. I ordered one for my sister in law, who wants to wear dresses but has a hard time finding any she likes. She loves this dress! She wears biker shorts underneath so her thighs don't rub, and it looks so good on her! She is 5'4, 180, and has a 34DD chest. It was a total win for someone who has had a hard time finding a dress she likes and is comfortable and not too short! I cannot recommend enough ☺️

Height raw: 5'1"

User comment: Pretty, if it fits My daughter and I are looking at dresses for her summer wedding. We love the soft buttery yellow of this dress. The fabric feels high quality and flows. All the layers are nicely hemmed, not serged. Good luck in trying to fit smaller people though. Most of our wedding party are smaller people. This is a size Small, the smallest it comes, and it fits me at 5'1" and 155#. I'm 50+, and tend to wear size 8/10, medium. This is definitely NOT for smaller chests and needs a decent cup size to fill up the gathers on top. Although the dress looks like a good fit for me, it felt uncomfortable at the waist, but that could just be my menopausal shape lol. Note that due to the side zipper the top tier of the skirt is sewn into the seam. It didn't lie as nicely, but not very noticeable. This seems too young of a style for me, and all the other ladies are smaller, so it's going back.
